rationale: Both correctly diagnose the root cause (default core_model run-level forces dummy weights via #4354's extension to online serving, so full_model tests need --run-level=full_model), matching the thread's 'invalid' resolution. Y is far more grounded, citing specific files and line numbers across the fixture/config/assertion chain and cross-referencing the gpu_memory_utilization split visible in the reporter's own debug log, whereas X's citations are vaguer (file/function names only) and its claim that the test itself carries an `@pytest.mark.full_model` marker is unverified filler. Y is also more complete, addressing hardware/video-path prerequisites and proposing a concrete footgun-prevention follow-up, which better serves the reporter's end-to-end situation.
